narrative: A 6.4 km connector between two well-known north-Srinagar localities, run by 3 distinct drivers, 6 times. Neither endpoint area is under-served overall: Hazratbal itself has 62 raw permit endpoints (it's one of the most heavily permitted areas in the whole registry) and Gulab Bagh has 2 (SRINAGAR to GULABAGH, City Bus class). No single raw permit runs this EXACT Gulab Bagh <-> Hazratbal pair by name, so this reads as a genuine informal local connector stitching together two already-served hubs, not a coverage gap. Geometrically clean and study-area-clipped inside Kashmir Division.
evidence_used: raw-permit registry cross-check (614 permits, 0 exact-name match, but both endpoint areas independently well-permitted)
data_quality_flags: 6 runs / 3 drivers is below the Tier-1 corridor confidence bar
no observed-stop cross-check performed (thin support doesn't justify the extra Nominatim/stop-matching pass)
recommendation: Plausible informal shortcut between two hubs, not a service gap. Not actionable at this support level; revisit if driver adoption in north Srinagar grows.
analyst: inline review, Opus (Claude), same session as Tier-1 audit
